title: Uncontrolled version sprawl
summary: Multiple profile-normalization versions now coexist, but compatibility and lifecycle rules are not governed by one runtime model.

services.summary: Original normalization behavior still used by legacy consumers.
Newer normalization behavior with different field interpretation and enrichment rules.
Still depends on older profile semantics.

runtime_decisions: Resolved version selection through consumer-specific conventions instead of explicit compatibility rules.
Could not prove safe coexistence between v1 and v2 behaviors.
Flagged the capability set as fragmented because version growth is no longer governed.
expectations: Versioning is necessary in evolving systems, but unmanaged versioning turns continuity into fragmentation.
The problem is not that multiple versions exist; it is that nothing explicitly governs how they coexist.
At this point the system is still functional, but behavior is no longer explained by one coherent model.
